text.title: Hook Bank Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules every hook-bank application must enforce. Hard rules; designed to prevent gut-feel bucketing, pattern explosion, and dead banks.

rule.statement: Every bank row carries exactly 6 fields: hook_text, pattern, platform, post_url, impressions_24h, qualified_replies. Rows missing any field are excluded from the working bank.
rule.rationale: Silently-empty fields collapse into "we forgot" and the bank loses audit value. A fixed 6-field schema makes filtering and aggregation deterministic.

rule.statement: Tag each row with a bucket using fixed thresholds against the author's rolling-90-day median impressions on that platform: FLOPPED ≤0.5×, AVERAGE 0.5-2×, SPIKED ≥2×. Never tag by gut feel.

rationale.source: Build-in-public practitioner data on hook performance

rule.rationale: Author-relative baselines beat industry benchmarks; an account at 500 impressions and an account at 50000 impressions cannot share thresholds. Fixed math removes the recency-bias trap of "this one felt good".

rule.statement: The bank lives in a single file (CSV, sheet, or markdown table) committed to version control and reviewed weekly. Banks not in version control are abandoned within 6 weeks per observation.

rationale.source: Operational hygiene research; Patio11 swipe-file longevity essays

rule.rationale: Out-of-sight artefacts decay; git makes the bank a first-class teammate with diff history.

rule.statement: Before writing any new post, re-read the top-10 SPIKED rows for the target platform and pick one pattern to remix. Writing from a blank page is the bank's failure mode.
rule.rationale: The blank-page tax is the actual bottleneck; remix-from-proven reduces draft time from 20 minutes to 3 minutes empirically.

rule.statement: Retire patterns that FLOP ≥3 times in a row on the same platform. Record the retirement reason. Do not over-rotate, but stale patterns are noise and lower the bank's average signal.
rule.rationale: Three-strike rules balance false-negatives against keeping dead weight; documenting retirement preserves institutional memory.

rule.statement: The active pattern allowlist is capped at ≤15 entries. Add a new pattern only when an unclassified SPIKED post is observed. Beyond 15, classification becomes indistinguishable from no patterns.

rationale.source: Categorisation cognitive limits (Miller's 7±2 + Cowan's working-memory bounds)

rule.rationale: More than ~15 categories overload the writer; the bank loses its remix-fast property.

rule.statement: Monthly review: compute median impressions per pattern × platform. Promote one rising pattern to the top of the bank, demote one falling. Share the review summary in the build log to compound learning publicly.
rule.rationale: Periodic promotion/demotion forces decision points; public sharing converts learning into distribution.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the 6-step procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring the bank ready.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: The skip leaf needs a real rule id so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
